app.name: Instantly
app.bestFor: High-volume senders and agencies running unlimited inboxes on a tight budget, with strong deliverability tooling.
app.rankBasis: Consistently placed in the top tier of 2026 cold-email comparisons from Salesforge, Hunter and Snov.io, with strong agency mindshare for unlimited sending accounts and deliverability tooling.
app.pricingPaid: Growth $47/mo — unlimited email accounts, 5,000 uploaded contacts and 10,000 emails/mo; trial available
features: AI sequence writing and reply management
pros: reviewers value unlimited inbox connections for agency-scale sending
fast campaign setup with integrated warmup
strong value at high mailbox counts
cons: no permanent free plan
lead and email limits still require upgrades at volume
users report that analytics and CRM depth lag specialist systems

app.name: Smartlead
app.bestFor: Agency-scale cold email with white-label client portals and unlimited inboxes on every plan.
app.rankBasis: A recurring top-three cold-email platform in Salesforge, Amplemarket and Saleshandy comparisons, especially cited for agency client management and unlimited mailboxes.
app.pricingPaid: Basic $39/mo — unlimited email accounts, 2,000 active leads and 6,000 emails/mo; trial available
pros: strong agency and white-label workflows
unlimited mailboxes on the entry plan
useful sender rotation and infrastructure controls
cons: no standing free plan
active-lead and send caps are low on the basic tier
interface and reporting can feel technical for first-time users

app.name: Lemlist
app.bestFor: Visual personalization — personalized images, GIFs, and video built into the sequence builder.
app.rankBasis: A long-running top cold-outreach recommendation across 2026 comparisons, differentiated by personalized images/video and backed by one of the category's larger independent-review communities.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: limited lead database and email-finder/verifier credits; campaign sending is not included
app.pricingPaid: Email Pro $79/user/mo — 3 sending emails/user, 150 free emails/day and personalization features
features: personalized images, video and landing pages
pros: best-in-class visual personalization workflows
reviewers praise sequence flexibility and multichannel outreach
large template, integration and community ecosystem
cons: substantially more expensive than volume-first competitors
mailbox and seat pricing gets costly for agencies
feature breadth creates a steeper setup curve

app.name: Apollo.io
app.bestFor: Combining prospecting and outreach in one tool with a 275M+ contact database.
app.rankBasis: One of the most frequently included sales-engagement platforms in major cold-email roundups, with a very large G2 review footprint and a proprietary B2B database that pure senders do not match.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: basic prospecting, 2 sequences and limited email/mobile credits
app.pricingPaid: Basic from $49/user/mo billed annually — advanced search, enrichment credits and expanded sequences
pros: combines prospect data and outreach in one system
large database and strong filtering
broad CRM and sales-tool integrations
cons: contact data accuracy varies by region and segment
credit rules and plan entitlements are complex
less specialized deliverability control than cold-email-only tools

app.name: Saleshandy
app.bestFor: High-volume senders scaling AI outreach cheaply, bundling an 852M+ B2B lead database.
app.rankBasis: A regular value pick in 2026 cold-email comparisons, with solid independent reviews for high sending allowances and a large bundled lead database but less mindshare than the top three.
app.pricingPaid: Outreach Starter $36/mo billed annually — unlimited email accounts, 5,000 prospects and 30,000 emails/mo; trial available
pros: high sending limits for the entry price
unlimited account connections suit small agencies
straightforward campaign and inbox workflow
cons: no permanent free plan
advanced agency and automation features require higher tiers
database coverage and enrichment quality are inconsistent in niche markets

app.name: Snov.io
app.bestFor: Startups on a tight budget wanting email finder, verifier, drip campaigns, and a CRM in one low-cost plan.
app.rankBasis: Frequently recommended as a lower-cost all-in-one option in Snov.io and independent sales-tool comparisons, supported by a broad user base for finder, verifier, CRM and campaigns.
app.pricingPaid: Starter $39/mo — 1,000 credits, 5,000 recipients and 3 mailbox warmups; trial includes 50 credits and 100 recipients
pros: broad prospecting-to-outreach toolkit in one subscription
accessible pricing for startups
useful browser extension and domain search
cons: credit consumption across tools can be difficult to forecast
CRM and analytics are basic compared with dedicated products
contact accuracy still requires verification and cleanup

app.name: Clay
app.bestFor: AI-driven prospect research and data enrichment to power deep personalization at scale.
app.rankBasis: A leading enrichment and workflow platform repeatedly featured in AI-outreach comparisons, with exceptional GTM-community mindshare even though it is an upstream research tool rather than a native email sender.
app.pricingPaid: Launch from $167/mo — 15,000 actions/mo and expanded enrichment/workflow capacity
pros: unusually flexible enrichment and research workflows
waterfall providers improve data coverage
excellent for generating deeply personalized outbound inputs
cons: not a complete sending platform by itself
usage-based actions can become expensive and hard to forecast
table and automation design has a significant learning curve

skill.name: marketingskills / cold-email (Corey Haines)
skill.bestFor: The de-facto leader for the cold-outreach craft: a SKILL.md that writes B2B cold emails and multi-touch follow-up sequences (subject lines, openers, CTAs, cadence). Lives in the most-adopted marketing Agent Skills repo (~41.5k stars). Writes copy — does not send.
skill.rankBasis: The cold-email skill sits in the category's dominant marketing Agent Skills repository at roughly 42k GitHub stars, giving it far more adoption and citation than any dedicated outreach prompt bundle.
pros: clear, practical craft guidance from a widely adopted repository
covers full sequences rather than one-off messages
vendor-neutral and easy to customize
cons: writes copy but does not find leads, send mail or track replies
repository-level stars do not measure this skill independently
generated claims and personalization still require human review

skill.name: Mautic
skill.bestFor: The recognized open-source marketing-automation platform (~10.2k stars); its campaign/drip builder drives multi-step email sequences. Built for nurture more than pure cold prospecting, so it is adapted for outreach rather than cold-native.
skill.rankBasis: A recognized open-source marketing-automation platform with roughly 10k GitHub stars and a long-standing enterprise/community ecosystem, though its reputation is for nurture automation rather than cold outreach.
skill.pricingPaid: Managed Mautic hosting is sold by third parties; no single official hosted entry price
pros: mature, extensible marketing-automation platform
full data ownership and deep campaign logic
large integration and implementation ecosystem
cons: heavy to install, upgrade and operate
designed for consent-based nurture, not mailbox-based cold sending
deliverability infrastructure and compliance remain the operator's responsibility

skill.name: listmonk
skill.bestFor: The highest-star self-hosted bulk email / campaign sender (~22.4k stars). Newsletter and opt-in oriented rather than cold-native, so it is a strong sending backend but not a sequencing or copywriting tool — hence medium for cold outreach.
skill.rankBasis: At roughly 22k GitHub stars it is the most-adopted self-hosted bulk campaign sender in this set, but community recognition centers on newsletters and opt-in lists rather than cold sequences.
pros: fast and resource-efficient at large list sizes
simple deployment and clean administration UI
full control over data and sending infrastructure
cons: no native cold-email sequencing or mailbox rotation
SMTP reputation, bounces and compliance are self-managed
best suited to opted-in broadcasts rather than prospecting

skill.name: BillionMail
skill.bestFor: A self-hosted mail server plus marketing/newsletter stack (~15.4k stars) for sending on your own domain and IP at scale. Sending infrastructure and deliverability, not cold sequencing or copy.
skill.rankBasis: A fast-growing self-hosted mail-server and marketing project at roughly 15k GitHub stars, notable for bundling infrastructure and campaigns but still young relative to mature mail stacks.
pros: combines mail infrastructure and campaign tooling
avoids per-contact SaaS pricing
active project with strong early adoption
cons: operating a mail server carries significant security and deliverability work
not purpose-built for personalized cold sequences
you own IP reputation, abuse handling and compliance

skill.name: OpenOutreach
skill.bestFor: A purpose-built self-hosted AI cold-outreach agent (~2.5k stars) that finds ICP leads, resolves work emails and sends personalized outreach from your own mailbox. The closest OSS fit for an end-to-end cold pipeline, but younger and smaller than the platforms above.
skill.rankBasis: The closest purpose-built open cold-outreach agent in the set at roughly 2.5k GitHub stars; promising adoption, but far smaller and less proven than general mail platforms.
skill.pricingFree: Free & open-source (self-host); external model, search and email-provider costs may apply
pros: covers more of the end-to-end cold workflow than most OSS alternatives
self-hosted control over lead and campaign data
agentic research can improve personalization
cons: you must configure several external services and credentials
young project with limited production-scale evidence
automated lead sourcing and sending require close compliance oversight

skill.name: coldoutboundskills
skill.bestFor: A 29-skill cold-outbound SKILL.md bundle (~559 stars) built from 1,000+ real B2B campaigns, covering strategy, deliverability setup, lead-gen, copywriting and campaign optimization — the deepest dedicated cold-outbound skill set after Corey Haines'.
skill.rankBasis: A dedicated 29-skill outbound bundle with roughly 560 GitHub stars and a claimed basis in 1,000+ campaigns, making it the deepest focused playbook set after the much larger marketingskills repo.
pros: unusually comprehensive cold-outbound coverage
practical playbooks span setup through optimization
free, modular and vendor-neutral
cons: skills provide guidance rather than working sender integrations
performance claims are difficult to verify independently
users must adapt tactics to current law and platform policies

skill.name: LeadMagic GTM Skills
skill.bestFor: The largest open catalog of GTM / cold-outbound SKILL.md playbooks (200+ playbooks covering outbound, enrichment, ICP scoring, ABM), but minimal GitHub adoption so far (~26 stars) — broad on paper, unproven, hence low confidence.
skill.rankBasis: More than 200 GTM playbooks provide unusual breadth, but roughly 25 GitHub stars and little independent discussion indicate minimal real-world adoption so far.
pros: very broad catalog for GTM teams
modular playbooks cover more than email copy
free and inspectable
cons: almost no independent adoption evidence
quality and maintenance consistency are hard to assess across 200+ skills
playbooks do not provide a sending or data platform

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt and re-ranked by real quality/reputation/adoption (verified GitHub stars, de-facto status) rather than by form factor — 'kind' is a tag only. It now mixes genuine SKILL.md Agent Skills and notable open-source repos. Corey Haines' marketingskills cold-email skill leads (~41.5k stars) as the recognized cold-outreach craft resource. Honest caveat: the highest-star repos here (listmonk, BillionMail) are newsletter/sending infrastructure, and Mautic is nurture-oriented automation, so none is truly cold-native — they are marked medium for this use case and pair with an app-track cold-email platform for sending. OpenOutreach is the closest purpose-built OSS cold-outreach tool. No MCP servers; the prior MCP entries were removed as out of scope.
